source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In the circuit shown below, each of the resistors has the same resistance.<image_1>A voltmeter with very high resistance is connected between two points in the circuit.Between which two points of connection would the voltmeter read zero?

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'In the circuit shown below' and asks about points in the diagram
the target points (e.g. P, Q, S, T, U, W) are only meaningful via the circuit image
multi_step: solving requires identifying electrical relationships between multiple labeled points in a resistor network, not just reading a label
the question asks which pair gives zero voltmeter reading, implying comparison of potentials across candidate point pairs
all resistors being equal suggests use of symmetry/equipotential reasoning or circuit analysis over more than one element
multiple-choice format with 4 candidate pairs supports a moderate multi-step reasoning load
verifiability: answer_type is short_text and the provided answer 'Q and U' is precise
choices are provided, so the answer can be directly matched to option A
the circuit claim can in principle be checked by a downstream verifier using the diagram and circuit rules
